  "text": "The type inference and overload resolution pipeline in dotnet/roslyn (v4.12.0) is implemented in files under src/Compilers/CSharp/Portable/Binder/Semantics/.\n\n1. **OverloadResolution** (OverloadResolution/OverloadResolution.cs): The main class orchestrating overload resolution. Contains key methods like MethodInvocationOverloadResolution (the primary entry point for resolving method calls) and BetterFunctionMember (which compares two candidate methods to determine which is better per the C# spec §12.6.4.3).\n\n2. **OverloadResolution_ArgsToParameters** (OverloadResolution/OverloadResolution_ArgsToParameters.cs): A partial class of OverloadResolution handling argument-to-parameter mapping, including named arguments and params arrays.\n\n3. **MethodTypeInferrer** (OverloadResolution/MethodTypeInference.cs): Performs generic method type argument inference (C# spec §12.6.3). Contains the Infer method and phase logic (first/second phase inference, fixing type variables).\n\n4. **Conversions** (Conversions/Conversions.cs) and **ConversionsBase** (Conversions/ConversionsBase.cs): The Conversions class (inheriting from ConversionsBase) classifies implicit and explicit conversions between types. Used extensively during overload resolution to determine applicable candidates and rank them. Key methods include ClassifyImplicitConversionFromType and ClassifyConversion.\n\n5. **BestTypeInferrer** (BestTypeInferrer.cs): Infers the best common type for a set of expressions (used for array creation expressions, conditional expressions, switch expressions, etc.). Contains InferBestType which applies the better conversion rules.\n\nSupporting types include: AnalyzedArguments (holds the analyzed argument list), MemberResolutionResult/MemberAnalysisResult (store per-candidate analysis results), OverloadResolutionResult (the final result), ArgumentAnalysisResult/ArgumentAnalysisResultKind (argument compatibility analysis), MethodGroup (the set of candidate methods), and the various Conversion/UserDefined conversion types in the Conversions subdirectory."
}
